gestures.mdmPrompt: The tutor leans slightly forward, opens both arms to invite attention, and emphasizes the topic clearly.
The tutor steps forward, points towards an imaginary example while clearly outlining the number of bags and apples.
The tutor shifts weight, raises the right hand with fingers extended, counting off the operation decisions for engagement.
The tutor stands tall, gestures outward with both hands, and confidently asks the student to explain multiplication.
The tutor leans in slightly, opens both hands towards the students, inviting them to rethink the concept clearly.
The tutor steps back a little, waves both arms to gather interest, and presents the next problem enthusiastically.
The tutor shifts to one side, lifts the right hand with fingers extended, rhythmically counting the steps to solve the multiplication.
The tutor stands confidently, gestures to both sides, and invites the student to explain multiplication once more.
